title: Hymn I.

source.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Fifteen Hymns For the Feast of the Epiphany.
verses.text: Hymns for the Feast of the Epiphany.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Fifteen Hymns For the Feast of the Epiphany.
verses.text: Resp.—To Thee be praise from Thy flock in the day of Thy Epiphany!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Fifteen Hymns For the Feast of the Epiphany.
verses.text: The heavens He has renewed, for that fools worshipped all the luminaries:—He has renewed the earth, for that in Adam it was wasted.—That which He fashioned has become new by His spittle:—and the All-Sufficing has restored bodies with souls.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Fifteen Hymns For the Feast of the Epiphany.
verses.text: Gather yourselves again ye—sheep and without labour receive cleansing!—for one needs not as Elisha—to bathe seven times in the river, nor again to be wearied as the priests are wearied with sprinklings.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Fifteen Hymns For the Feast of the Epiphany.
verses.text: Seven times Elisha purified himself in a mystery of the seven spirits;—and the hyssop and blood are a mighty symbol.—There is no room for division;—He is not divided from the Lord of all Who is Son of the Lord of all.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Fifteen Hymns For the Feast of the Epiphany.
verses.text: Moses sweetened in Marah the waters that were bitter,—because the People complained and murmured:—Thus he gave a sign of baptism,—wherein the Lord of life makes sweet them that were bitter.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Fifteen Hymns For the Feast of the Epiphany.
verses.text: The cloud overshadowed and kept off the burning heat from the camp;—it showed a symbol of the Holy Spirit, which overshadows you in baptism—tempering the flaming fire that it harm not your bodies.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Fifteen Hymns For the Feast of the Epiphany.
verses.text: Through the sea the People then passed, and showed a symbol—of the baptism wherein ye were washed.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Fifteen Hymns For the Feast of the Epiphany.
verses.text: The People passed through that and believed not:—the Gentiles were baptized in this and believed and received the Holy Ghost.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Fifteen Hymns For the Feast of the Epiphany.
verses.text: The Word sent the Voice to proclaim before His Coming,—to prepare for Him the way by which He came,—and to betroth the Bride till He should come,—that she might be ready when He should come and take her from the water.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Fifteen Hymns For the Feast of the Epiphany.
verses.text: The voice of prophecy stirred the son of the barren woman,—and he went forth wandering in the desert and crying,—“Lo! the Son of the Kingdom comes!—prepare ye the way that He may enter and abide in your dwellings!”

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Fifteen Hymns For the Feast of the Epiphany.
verses.text: John cried, “Who comes after me, He is before me:—I am the Voice but not the Word;—I am the torch but not the Light;—the Star that rises before the Sun of Righteousness.”

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Fifteen Hymns For the Feast of the Epiphany.
verses.text: In the wilderness this John had cried and had said,—“Repent ye sinners of your evils,—and offer the fruits of repentance;—for lo!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Fifteen Hymns For the Feast of the Epiphany.
verses.text: He comes that winnows the wheat from the tares.”

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Fifteen Hymns For the Feast of the Epiphany.
verses.text: The Lightgiver has prevailed and marked a mystery, by the degrees he ascended:—Lo! there are twelve days since he ascended,—and to-day this is the thirteenth:—a perfect mystery of Him, the Son, and His twelve!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Fifteen Hymns For the Feast of the Epiphany.
verses.text: Darkness was overcome to make it manifest that Satan was overcome;—and the Light prevailed that he should proclaim—that the Firstborn triumphs: darkness was overcome—with the Dark Spirit, and our Light prevailed with the Lightgiver.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Fifteen Hymns For the Feast of the Epiphany.
verses.text: In the Height and the Depth the Son had two heralds.—The star of light proclaimed Him from above;—John likewise preached Him from beneath:—two heralds, the earthly and the heavenly.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Fifteen Hymns For the Feast of the Epiphany.
verses.text: The star of light, contrary to nature, shone forth of a sudden;—less than the sun yet greater than the sun.—Less was it than he in manifest light;—and greater than he in secret might because of its mystery.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Fifteen Hymns For the Feast of the Epiphany.
verses.text: The star of light shed its rays among them that were in darkness,—and guided them as though they were blind;—so that they came and met the great Light:—they gave offerings and received life and adored and departed.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Fifteen Hymns For the Feast of the Epiphany.
verses.text: The herald from above showed His Nature to be from the Most High;—likewise he that was from beneath showed His Body to be from humankind, mighty marvel!—that His Godhead and His Manhood by them were proclaimed!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Fifteen Hymns For the Feast of the Epiphany.
verses.text: Thus whoso reckons Him as of earth, the star of light—will convince him that He is of Heaven: and whoso reckons Him as of spirit,—this John will convince him that He is also bodily.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Fifteen Hymns For the Feast of the Epiphany.
verses.text: John drew near with his parents and worshipped the Sun,—and brightness rested on His Face.—He was not moved as when in the womb.—Mighty marvel! that here he worships and there he leaped!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Fifteen Hymns For the Feast of the Epiphany.
verses.text: The whole creation became for Him as one mouth and cried out concerning Him.—The Magi cry out in their gifts;—the barren cry out with their children;—the star of light, lo! it cries out in the air, “Behold the Son of the King!”

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Fifteen Hymns For the Feast of the Epiphany.
verses.text: The heavens are opened, the waters break forth, the dove is in glory!—The voice of the Father is stronger than thunder,—as it utters the word, “This is My Beloved”;—the Watchers brought the tidings, the children acclaimed Him in their Hosannas.
